description: Autonomous AI orchestrator for FHI repos. Watches for ready beads, dispatches Claude Code workers in git worktrees, reviews output via a Warden agent, creates PRs, and monitors them through CI and review comments to completion. Native Windows, single Go binary, Cobra CLI + Bubbletea TUI.
title: The Forge - Autonomous AI Orchestrator

description: Initialize the Go module, create README.md with project overview and naming conventions (Hearth, Smith, Warden, Temper, Bellows, Anvil, Heat), set up .gitignore for Go binaries/build artifacts, and create the basic directory structure (cmd/forge/, internal/, docs/).
title: Initialize repo: README, .gitignore, go.mod

close_reason: Phase 0 complete: Cobra CLI, Viper config, SQLite state, anvil commands, bd poller
description: Go module with Cobra CLI skeleton, config system (forge.yaml with anvil definitions), bd integration for polling ready beads, SQLite state database. Foundation that all other phases build on.
title: Phase 0: Foundation

close_reason: Cobra CLI skeleton: rootCmd, version, signal context, command groups, persistent flags
description: Set up cmd/forge/main.go with Cobra rootCmd, version command, and command group registration pattern matching beads. Include PersistentPreRun for config loading and signal-aware context.
title: Cobra CLI skeleton with root command

close_reason: Config system: Viper-based forge.yaml loading, AnvilConfig/SettingsConfig structs, env overrides, validation, Save for anvil commands
description: Implement config loading using Viper. Support forge.yaml in working directory or ~/.forge/config.yaml. Define AnvilConfig struct (path, max_smiths) and SettingsConfig (poll_interval, smith_timeout, max_total_smiths, claude_flags). Support env var overrides with FORGE_ prefix.
title: Config system: forge.yaml with Viper

close_reason: Anvil commands: add/remove/list with .beads validation, config persistence
description: Implement anvil management commands. 'forge anvil add <name> <path>' registers a repo by writing to forge.yaml. 'forge anvil list' shows configured repos with status. 'forge anvil remove <name>' deregisters. Validate that path contains .beads/ directory.
title: forge anvil add/remove/list commands

close_reason: BeadPoller: polls bd ready --json across anvils, merges by priority, forge queue command
description: Implement BeadPoller that runs 'bd ready --json --unassigned' in each anvil directory, parses IssueWithCounts JSON, merges results sorted by priority, and returns a unified queue. Handle bd connection errors gracefully per-anvil.
title: bd integration: poll ready beads across anvils

close_reason: SQLite state DB: workers, prs, events tables with CRUD, WAL mode, modernc.org/sqlite
description: Create state.db at ~/.forge/state.db with tables: workers (id, bead_id, anvil, branch, pid, status, started_at, completed_at, log_path), prs (number, anvil, bead_id, branch, status, created_at, last_checked), events (timestamp, type, message, bead_id, anvil). Use modernc.org/sqlite for pure Go driver.
title: SQLite state database

description: Pre-PR review agent (Warden) that inspects Smith output, runs build/test/lint (Temper), feedback loop for Smith retries on rejection, PR creation via gh CLI, worktree cleanup after PR.
title: Phase 2: Warden Pipeline

close_reason: Temper: auto-detect Go/.NET/Node, sequential step execution, per-step timeouts, optional steps, structured results
description: Implement Temper as pre-Warden check. Run build/lint/test in worktree without Claude. API anvils: dotnet build && dotnet test. Client: npm run test:run && npm run lint. Parse exit codes. Fast fail before spending tokens on Warden review.
title: Temper: build/lint/test verification

description: Implement Warden: spawn separate Claude session on completed Smith branch. Prompt includes git diff vs origin/main, instruction to check AGENTS.md conventions, verify tests pass, verify changelog fragments. Claude outputs structured verdict (pass/fail + reasons) via --output-format json.
title: Warden review agent

description: When Warden rejects, spawn new Smith on same branch with Warden feedback as prompt context. Loop up to max_review_attempts (default 2). Track attempt count in state.db. After max attempts mark bead as needs_human and emit event. Do not clean up worktree on rejection.
title: Smith-Warden feedback loop

description: After Warden passes, create PR via gh pr create --title --body --base main in worktree. Body includes bead ID reference. Close bead via bd close <id> --reason PR created. Add PR to watched-prs in state.db. Clean up worktree after PR creation.
title: PR creation via gh CLI

close_reason: Phase 1 complete: worktree, smith, prompt, worker FSM, watchdog
description: Worktree creation via bd worktree, Claude Code CLI invocation with prompt templates, worker lifecycle state machine (queued/spawning/running/completed/failed), process timeout and cleanup, BD_ACTOR identity setup.
title: Phase 1: Smith Pipeline

close_reason: WorktreeManager: create/teardown git worktrees at .workers/<bead-id>/, branch forge/<bead-id> from origin/main, .beads/redirect installed
description: Implement WorktreeManager that creates worktrees via 'bd worktree create .workers/<bead-id> --branch feature/<bead-id>-<slug>' in the anvil directory. Track worktree paths. Implement cleanup via 'bd worktree remove'. Handle git fetch origin before branch creation.
title: Worktree manager using bd worktree

close_reason: SmithProcess: spawns claude CLI with stream-json, captures stdout/stderr, extracts cost/tokens, logs sessions
description: Implement SmithSpawner that executes 'claude -p <prompt> --dangerously-skip-permissions --output-format json --max-turns 50' as a child process using os/exec. Set cmd.Dir to worktree path. Capture stdout/stderr to log files at ~/.forge/logs/<bead-id>.log. Set BD_ACTOR=forge/smith/<bead-id> env var.
title: Claude Code CLI process spawner

close_reason: Prompt Builder: templates from bead metadata + repo context, per-anvil overrides via .forge/prompt.tmpl
description: Create prompt templates for Smith workers. Template includes: bead ID, title, description, branch name, repo conventions reference (AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md), explicit instructions for commit/push/changelog. Support Go text/template for customization. Store templates in ~/.forge/templates/ with built-in defaults.
title: Prompt template system

close_reason: Worker FSM: pending->running->done/failed, integrates worktree+smith+prompt+stateDB, enforces per-anvil and global worker limits
description: Implement WorkerPool with state machine: queued -> spawning -> running -> completed | failed | timeout. Track active workers per anvil against max_smiths limit. Poll Process.Wait() in goroutines. On completion check branch was pushed via 'git ls-remote'. Update state.db. Emit events.
title: Worker lifecycle state machine

close_reason: Watchdog: periodic timeout checks, graceful then forceful kill, kill-all on shutdown, state DB event logging
description: Implement smith_timeout (default 30min wall clock). Kill process on timeout via cmd.Process.Kill(). Clean up worktree. Mark bead status back to open after configurable cooldown for retry. Log timeout events. Ensure no orphaned processes on unexpected exit.
title: Process timeout and cleanup

description: PR polling loop for CI status and review comments, CI fix workers that address failing checks, review comment fix workers that address reviewer feedback, PR lifecycle state machine tracking.
title: Phase 3: Bellows - PR Monitor

description: Implement BellowsMonitor polling every 10min per anvil: gh pr list --state open --json. Query unresolved review threads via GraphQL (isResolved:false). Update PR status in state.db.
title: PR polling loop for CI and reviews

description: When PR has failing CI, checkout branch in new worktree, spawn Claude with failure logs. Prompt: PR has failing checks with logs, fix and push. Reuse Smith/Warden pipeline. Limit to max_ci_fix_attempts (default 2) per PR.
title: CI fix workers

description: When PR has unresolved review threads, checkout branch in worktree, spawn Claude with review comments. After push, resolve threads via GraphQL resolveReviewThread mutation. Limit to max_review_fix_attempts per PR.
title: Review comment fix workers

description: State machine per PR: created -> ci_passing -> reviewed -> needs_fix -> fixing -> ci_passing. Track in state.db with timestamps. Emit events on transitions. Show in TUI. Remove PR from monitoring after merge detected via gh pr view --json state.
title: PR lifecycle state tracking

description: Bubbletea TUI dashboard with three panels (queue, workers, event log). Keyboard navigation, lipgloss styling with color-coded status, problems view for stuck workers. Accessible via forge hearth command.
title: Phase 4: Hearth - TUI Dashboard

description: Create Bubbletea app with three-panel lipgloss layout: Queue (left), Workers (center), Event Log (right). Tab switches panel focus, j/k scroll. Connect to daemon state via named pipe or state.db polling.
title: Bubbletea app scaffold with three panels

description: Left panel shows ready beads across all anvils sorted by priority. Display: bead ID, title, priority (color-coded), anvil name, status. Auto-refresh from daemon state.
title: Queue panel: ready beads display

description: Center panel shows active Smiths, Wardens, Bellows workers. Display: worker type icon, bead ID, anvil, elapsed time, last log line. Color-coded: green=running, yellow=reviewing, red=failed. K key kills selected worker.
title: Workers panel: active sessions

description: Right panel shows chronological events from state.db: Smith started, Warden passed/failed, PR created, CI failed, Bellows fixing. Timestamps, color-coded by type. Auto-scroll with manual scroll lock.
title: Event log panel

description: Full CLI command set: forge up/down (daemon lifecycle), forge hearth (TUI), forge status, forge anvil add/remove/list, forge history, forge doctor, forge config. Daemon architecture with named pipe for TUI connection.
title: Phase 5: CLI Commands and Daemon

description: Implement daemon as background process. forge up starts it (PID to ~/.forge/forge.pid). forge down sends graceful shutdown. Daemon runs main loop: bead poller, worker pool, Bellows monitor. Logs to ~/.forge/logs/daemon.log.
title: Daemon process: forge up/down

description: Implement named pipe (Windows) or Unix socket (Linux) for TUI to connect to running daemon. Protocol: JSON messages. Stream: worker state updates, events, queue changes. Allow TUI to send commands: kill worker, refresh.
title: Named pipe IPC for TUI

description: forge status: one-shot state dump as table or --json. forge history: completed beads log from state.db. forge doctor: health checks (bd installed, gh authenticated, claude installed, anvils valid, daemon running, state.db accessible).
title: forge status/history/doctor commands

description: Retry logic with exponential backoff, cost tracking from Claude JSON output, graceful shutdown, Windows Task Scheduler integration, optional Teams webhook notifications, config hot-reload.
title: Phase 6: Polish and Resilience

description: Failed beads retried up to max_retries (default 2) with exponential backoff (5min, 15min, 45min). After max retries mark needs_human in state.db. Track retry count per bead.
title: Retry logic with exponential backoff

description: Parse Claude --output-format json for token usage (input/output/cache tokens). Store per-bead and per-day aggregates in state.db. Show in forge status and TUI. Optional configurable daily cost limit that pauses new work.
title: Cost tracking from Claude output

description: On forge down, SIGTERM, SIGINT: let active Smiths finish current turn (configurable grace), then kill. Clean up all worktrees. Update state.db. Ensure no orphaned claude processes. On startup detect and clean stale workers from previous crash.
title: Graceful shutdown and orphan prevention

description: Document and optionally implement auto-startup via Windows Task Scheduler. forge doctor suggests the setup. Create XML task definition for forge up at user login. Alternative: Windows Service via golang.org/x/sys/windows/svc.
title: Windows Task Scheduler auto-start

description: Optional Teams incoming webhook integration. Notifications: PR created, bead failed after retries, daily cost summary. Configure webhook URL in forge.yaml. Disable by default. Format as Adaptive Cards.
title: Teams webhook notifications

description: Watch forge.yaml for changes using fsnotify. Apply config changes to running daemon without restart: poll_interval, max_smiths, claude_flags. Log config changes. Anvil additions/removals require restart.
title: Config hot-reload
